binders.name: WH3 Flammable <25C
binders.description: Shared binder containing flammable liquids in Warehouse 3 with flash point below 25°C.

binders.name: Lab A Working Set
binders.description: Ad-hoc binder of frequently referenced SDS for Lab A experiments.

binders.name: Warehouse 1 High Hazard Chemicals
binders.description: Shared binder with Warehouse 1 chemicals that have multiple hazard pictograms or severe hazard classes.

binders.name: Production Line 2 SDS Review Set
binders.description: Binder used by EHS to stage SDS in scope for Production Line 2 periodic review.

chemical_substances.name: Acetone
chemical_substances.description: A highly volatile, flammable solvent commonly used in laboratories and industrial cleaning processes.

chemical_substances.name: Isopropyl alcohol
chemical_substances.description: A flammable solvent and disinfectant; primary component in many cleaning mixtures such as 'Lab Cleaning Mixture A'.

chemical_substances.name: Sodium hydroxide
chemical_substances.description: A strong inorganic base used in cleaning and process applications; corrosive to skin and eyes. Basis for 50% aqueous solution SDS.

chemical_substances.name: Ethanol
chemical_substances.description: A flammable solvent used in disinfectants, cleaning products, and as a process solvent.

chemical_substances.name: Methanol
chemical_substances.description: A toxic and flammable solvent; exposure can cause blindness or death.

chemical_substances.name: Toluene
chemical_substances.description: A volatile aromatic hydrocarbon used as an industrial solvent and in coatings; flammable liquid with CNS effects.

chemical_substances.name: Xylene
chemical_substances.description: A mixture of aromatic hydrocarbons used as a solvent in paints, coatings, and laboratory applications.

chemical_substances.name: Benzene
chemical_substances.description: A volatile aromatic hydrocarbon classified as a Category 1 carcinogen; commonly monitored in dashboards by building.

chemical_substances.name: Formaldehyde
chemical_substances.description: A reactive aldehyde used in resins and disinfectants; classified as a human carcinogen and respiratory sensitizer.

chemical_substances.name: Hydrochloric acid
chemical_substances.description: A strong inorganic acid commonly used in cleaning and pH adjustment; corrosive and fuming at higher concentrations.

chemical_substances.name: Sulfuric acid
chemical_substances.description: A strong mineral acid with severe corrosive properties; used in batteries, cleaning, and production processes.

chemical_substances.name: Nitric acid
chemical_substances.description: A strong oxidizing acid used in metal treatment and nitration reactions; highly corrosive and reactive.

chemical_substances.name: Acetic acid
chemical_substances.description: A corrosive organic acid used in cleaning, synthesis, and as a pH adjuster in various processes.

chemical_substances.name: N,N-Dimethylformamide
chemical_substances.description: A polar aprotic solvent with potential reproductive toxicity, used in specialty coatings and synthesis.

chemical_substances.name: Acetonitrile
chemical_substances.description: A polar solvent used in HPLC and organic synthesis; flammable and toxic by inhalation and ingestion.

chemical_substances.name: Potassium hydroxide
chemical_substances.description: A strong base similar to sodium hydroxide, used in cleaning agents and chemical manufacturing; corrosive.

chemical_substances.name: Hydrogen peroxide
chemical_substances.description: An oxidizing agent used in bleaching and disinfecting solutions; concentrated solutions are corrosive and reactive.

chemical_substances.name: Sodium hypochlorite
chemical_substances.description: An oxidizing and corrosive component of many cleaning and disinfecting products, especially custodial-grade bleaches.

favorites_lists.name: Lab A Favorites
favorites_lists.description: Pinned SDS frequently used in Lab A, including the most recent Acetone SDS.

favorites_lists.name: Lab B Favorites
favorites_lists.description: Favorites list for Lab B reference chemicals and cleaning agents.

favorites_lists.name: Custodial Team Essentials
favorites_lists.description: Key SDS for daily-use cleaning and disinfection chemicals used by the custodial team.

lists.name: SDS Review - Next 90 Days
lists.description: Review list of SDS in Production Line 2 that are scheduled to expire within the next 90 days.

lists.name: Emergency Response Kit - Floor 2
lists.description: Set of top NFPA health-rated chemicals on Building B Floor 2 that must be included in the emergency response kit.

lists.name: Emergency Response Kit - Warehouse 1
lists.description: Core chemicals and neutralizing agents to be staged near Warehouse 1 for spill response.

lists.name: Building A Carcinogen Watchlist
lists.description: Generic monitoring list for Category 1 carcinogenic substances stored in Building A.

lists.name: Production Line 2 Process Chemicals
lists.description: Generic list of core process chemicals used on Production Line 2, used as a basis for dashboards and reviews.

lists.name: Custodial Daily Use Chemicals
lists.description: List of detergents, disinfectants, and corrosive cleaners to be checked during daily custodial inspections.

quick_access_groups.name: Custodial Team - Corrosives
quick_access_groups.description: Quick access group for corrosive cleaning chemicals with Spanish SDS used by the custodial team.

quick_access_groups.name: Lab A - Frequently Used
quick_access_groups.description: Quick access group with high-turnover solvents and reagents used daily in Lab A.

quick_access_groups.name: EHS Team - Priority Chemicals
quick_access_groups.description: Quick access to SDS for high hazard substances prioritized by the EHS team for oversight.

sds_revisions.summaryOfChanges: Initial issue of Acetone SDS aligned with GHS classification.

contentSections.title: Section 1: Identification
contentSections.content: Product identifier: Acetone. Recommended use: laboratory solvent.

contentSections.title: Section 2: Hazard Identification
contentSections.content: Highly flammable liquid and vapour. Causes serious eye irritation.

contentSections.title: Section 8: Exposure Controls/Personal Protection
contentSections.content: Use local exhaust ventilation. Wear safety goggles and nitrile gloves. Use organic vapour respirator if ventilation is inadequate.

sds_revisions.summaryOfChanges: Updated occupational exposure limits and spill response procedures.

contentSections.title: Section 2: Hazard Identification
contentSections.content: Flam. Liq. 2, Eye Irrit. 2. Signal word: Danger. Pictograms: Flame, Exclamation mark.

contentSections.title: Section 7: Handling and Storage
contentSections.content: Keep away from heat, hot surfaces, sparks, open flames and other ignition sources. Use explosion-proof equipment.

contentSections.title: Section 8: Exposure Controls/Personal Protection
contentSections.content: Use chemical splash goggles, nitrile gloves, and lab coat. If engineering controls are not sufficient, use an organic vapour respirator.

sds_revisions.summaryOfChanges: Added specific PPE recommendations for splash-prone operations and updated first-aid wording.

contentSections.title: Section 2: Hazard Identification
contentSections.content: GHS Classification unchanged. Added clarification on target organ effects from repeated exposure.

contentSections.title: Section 4: First Aid Measures
contentSections.content: Clarified eye rinsing duration (15 minutes minimum) and observation by medical personnel.

contentSections.title: Section 8: Exposure Controls/Personal Protection
contentSections.content: Maintain ventilation to keep vapours below exposure limits. Wear chemical splash goggles, flame-resistant lab coat, and nitrile gloves (0.4 mm or thicker). For transfer of bulk quantities in Lab A, use a face shield in addition to goggles.

sds_revisions.summaryOfChanges: Updated concentration range and neutralization guidance.

contentSections.title: Section 1: Identification
contentSections.content: Product name: Sodium hydroxide 50% solution.

contentSections.title: Section 2: Hazard Identification
contentSections.content: Causes severe skin burns and eye damage. Corrosion pictogram.

contentSections.title: Section 8: Exposure Controls/Personal Protection
contentSections.content: Wear chemical-resistant gloves and safety goggles. Provide eyewash stations and safety showers.

sds_revisions.summaryOfChanges: Aligned with updated corrosive classification and storage temperature range.

contentSections.title: Section 7: Handling and Storage
contentSections.content: Do not store in aluminium containers. Maintain temperature between 15–32 °C to prevent solidification.

contentSections.title: Section 8: Exposure Controls/Personal Protection
contentSections.content: Use tightly fitting safety goggles and chemical-resistant gloves (nitrile, neoprene). Wear long-sleeved chemical-resistant clothing when handling bulk quantities.

sds_revisions.summaryOfChanges: Clarified PPE recommendations and updated emergency exposure limits.

contentSections.title: Section 2: Hazard Identification
contentSections.content: Classification unchanged. Added note on respiratory irritation at high aerosol concentrations.

contentSections.title: Section 8: Exposure Controls/Personal Protection
contentSections.content:
Wear chemical splash goggles and face shield when there is a risk of splashing. Use chemical-resistant gloves (nitrile, neoprene; breakthrough time > 480 min). Wear long-sleeved chemical-resistant apron when handling drums.
Respiratory protection: Not normally required; use NIOSH-approved respirator if mists are generated.

sds_revisions.summaryOfChanges: Revised PPE guidance for face protection and added requirement for chemical-resistant sleeves in certain operations.

contentSections.title: Section 2: Hazard Identification
contentSections.content: No change in GHS classification. Added note: "Contact with metals may release flammable hydrogen gas."

contentSections.title: Section 8: Exposure Controls/Personal Protection
contentSections.content:
Engineering controls: Enclose transfer points and provide local exhaust for bulk transfers.
Eye/face protection: Chemical splash goggles plus full-face shield are mandatory for any open handling or drum transfer.
Skin protection: Wear chemical-resistant gloves (butyl rubber or nitrile, thickness ≥ 0.5 mm), chemical-resistant sleeves, and a long chemical-resistant apron. For potential large splashes, use full chemical suit.
Respiratory protection: Use NIOSH-approved respirator with P2/P3 particulate filter if aerosols or mists may form.

sds_revisions.summaryOfChanges: Initial SDS issued for Isopropyl alcohol.

contentSections.title: Section 1: Identification
contentSections.content: Product name: Isopropyl alcohol; CAS 67-63-0.

contentSections.title: Section 2: Hazard Identification
contentSections.content: Flammable liquid and vapour. Causes serious eye irritation. May cause drowsiness or dizziness.

contentSections.title: Section 8: Exposure Controls/Personal Protection
contentSections.content: Use safety goggles and compatible chemical-resistant gloves. Ensure good general ventilation.

sds_revisions.summaryOfChanges: Updated exposure limit values and storage recommendations.

contentSections.title: Section 7: Handling and Storage
contentSections.content: Store in a well-ventilated place. Keep cool. Keep container tightly closed.

contentSections.title: Section 8: Exposure Controls/Personal Protection
contentSections.content: Maintain airborne concentrations below national occupational exposure limits. Wear splash goggles and nitrile gloves when handling bulk quantities.

sds_revisions.summaryOfChanges: Minor editorial changes and clarification of PPE for spray applications.

contentSections.title: Section 2: Hazard Identification
contentSections.content: GHS classification unchanged. Added specific target organ toxicity (single exposure) Category 3 for narcotic effects.

contentSections.title: Section 8: Exposure Controls/Personal Protection
contentSections.content: For routine wiping and cleaning, wear safety glasses and nitrile gloves. For spray or aerosol applications, use splash goggles and organic vapour respirator.

sds_revisions.summaryOfChanges: Initial SDS for denatured ethanol used as disinfectant.

contentSections.title: Section 2: Hazard Identification
contentSections.content: Flammable liquid and vapour. Causes serious eye irritation.

contentSections.title: Section 8: Exposure Controls/Personal Protection
contentSections.content: Wear safety glasses and suitable gloves. Ensure adequate ventilation during bulk handling.

sds_revisions.summaryOfChanges: Updated flammability statements and added note on use in hand sanitizers.

contentSections.title: Section 1: Identification
contentSections.content: Product name: Ethanol (denatured). Recommended use: solvent, disinfectant.

contentSections.title: Section 8: Exposure Controls/Personal Protection
contentSections.content: For normal use, safety glasses and gloves are recommended. For large-scale transfers, use flame-resistant lab coat and ensure bonding and grounding of containers.

sds_revisions.summaryOfChanges: Initial SDS for sodium hypochlorite cleaning solution supplied to custodial team.

contentSections.title: Section 2: Hazard Identification
contentSections.content: Causes severe skin burns and eye damage. Very toxic to aquatic life. Corrosion and environment pictograms.

contentSections.title: Section 8: Exposure Controls/Personal Protection
contentSections.content: Wear protective gloves, eye protection, and protective clothing. Use in well-ventilated areas only.

sds_revisions.summaryOfChanges: Added Spanish language section and clarified mixing incompatibilities.

contentSections.title: Section 2: Hazard Identification
contentSections.content: Added Spanish hazard statements: "Provoca quemaduras graves en la piel y lesiones oculares graves."

contentSections.title: Section 8: Controles de exposición / protección personal
contentSections.content: Usar guantes resistentes a productos químicos, protección ocular con ajuste hermético y delantal resistente a productos químicos durante la manipulación a granel.

sds_revisions.summaryOfChanges: SDS emitida para limpiador alcalino corrosivo (versión en español).

contentSections.title: Sección 2: Identificación de los peligros
contentSections.content: Líquido corrosivo para metales. Provoca quemaduras graves en la piel y lesiones oculares graves.

contentSections.title: Sección 8: Controles de exposición / protección personal
contentSections.content: Guantes resistentes a productos químicos, gafas de seguridad con protección lateral, ropa de protección.

sds_revisions.summaryOfChanges: Actualización de equipo de protección para operaciones de alta exposición y límites de uso diario.

contentSections.title: Sección 2: Identificación de los peligros
contentSections.content: Clasificación GHS sin cambios. Añadida indicación de uso exclusivo para personal de limpieza entrenado.

contentSections.title: Sección 8: Controles de exposición / protección personal
contentSections.content: Uso diario típico: hasta 10 L/día por equipo. En operaciones de fregado intensivo, usar pantalla facial completa además de gafas ajustadas, guantes de nitrilo de manga larga y delantal resistente a productos químicos.

sds_revisions.summaryOfChanges: Initial SDS reflecting Category 1 carcinogen classification.

contentSections.title: Section 2: Hazard Identification
contentSections.content: Flammable liquid and vapour. May cause cancer. Causes damage to blood through prolonged or repeated exposure.

contentSections.title: Section 8: Exposure Controls/Personal Protection
contentSections.content: Use closed systems where possible. Wear chemical splash goggles, solvent-resistant gloves, and appropriate respirator where exposure limits may be exceeded.

sds_revisions.summaryOfChanges: Updated occupational exposure limits and added additional monitoring recommendations for Buildings A, B, and C.

contentSections.title: Section 7: Handling and Storage
contentSections.content: Store locked up in designated carcinogen cabinets in each building. Keep away from heat and ignition sources.

contentSections.title: Section 8: Exposure Controls/Personal Protection
contentSections.content: Implement routine air monitoring in areas where benzene is used. Use full-face respirator with organic vapour cartridges if engineering controls are insufficient. Maintain PPE records per building.
